# MCP Gateway

> Universal MCP gateway -- single port for all your MCP servers, ~95% context token savings. Rust.

MCP Gateway sits between your AI client and your MCP servers. Instead of loading hundreds of tool definitions into every request (burning ~150 tokens each), the AI gets 4 meta-tools and discovers the right one on demand. Like an app store for tools.

## Install

```bash
brew install MikkoParkkola/tap/mcp-gateway
```

Or: `cargo install mcp-gateway`

## Quick Start

1. Create `gateway.yaml`:

```yaml
server:
  port: 39400
meta_mcp:
  enabled: true
backends:
  - name: my-server
    transport: stdio
    command: ["my-mcp-server"]
```

2. Run: `mcp-gateway --config gateway.yaml`
3. Point your AI client at `http://localhost:39400/sse`

## How It Works

Your AI gets 4 meta-tools instead of hundreds:
- `gateway_search` -- find tools by keyword
- `gateway_invoke` -- call any discovered tool
- `gateway_list_tools` -- list tools from a specific backend
- `gateway_list_servers` -- list all configured backends

## Key Features

- Single-port multiplexing for unlimited MCP backends
- REST API integration via YAML capability files (no MCP server needed)
- Circuit breakers and retry policies per backend
- OpenAPI spec import (`mcp-gateway import openapi spec.yaml`)
- Session sandboxing and idempotency
- Semantic tool search (TF-IDF + SimHash dedup)
- 2500+ tests

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/MikkoParkkola/mcp-gateway
- Homebrew: `brew install MikkoParkkola/tap/mcp-gateway`
- Crates.io: https://crates.io/crates/mcp-gateway
